global_cues.us_markets: Wall Street ended lower on Apr 28: S&P 500 -0.49% to 7,138.80, Nasdaq -0.9% to 24,663.80, Dow -0.05% to 49,141.90. Selloff driven by WSJ report that OpenAI missed revenue goals (chip stocks hit) and rising oil prices.
global_cues.european_markets: European indices closed mixed/slightly higher on Apr 28; investors focused on Iran war developments and upcoming central bank meetings.
global_cues.asian_markets: Nikkei 225 fell 0.6% to below 60,200 after BoJ held rates at 0.75% (4th straight meeting) but raised inflation outlook and cut FY26 growth. SoftBank -10%, Advantest -5%, Tokyo Electron -4.1%. Asian markets broadly weak this morning amid oil surge.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs remain net sellers — sold ₹43,967 cr in April 2026 through Apr 21 (10th straight month of outflows, $4.4B in April alone). DIIs absorbing the pain: bought ₹29,696 cr in April so far (after ₹1.42 lakh cr in March). FII ownership in Nifty 50 at 13-15 year low (~24.1%). Yesterday's session: Sensex -417 pts to 76,886.91, Nifty -97 pts to 23,995.70 (below 24,000).
earnings_calendar: ~47 companies reporting today; 208 BSE/NSE firms reporting Apr 27-May 2
macro_events: RBI held repo rate at 5.25% with neutral stance (Apr 8 policy); FY27 CPI projection 4.6%, GDP 6.9%
March CPI inflation came in at 3.40% (up from 3.21% in Feb)
US Fed rate decision pending — investor caution
BoJ held at 0.75% (Apr 28), raised inflation outlook, cut FY26 growth on Middle East impact
Nifty monthly F&O expiry was yesterday (Apr 28); SAIL under F&O ban
sector_themes: PSU Banks weak — PSU Bank index fell ~2% yesterday; SBI among top losers
Auto under pressure from rising fuel costs hurting demand; GST cuts of 5-10% support entry/premium segments
IT mixed: weak rupee tailwind for exporters, but Wipro -2.83% post Q4; sector facing derating
Pharma corrected on energy/freight cost pressures (freight up 15-30%); margin concerns
Banking resilient on stable domestic fundamentals; Nifty Bank gained 478 pts (+0.85%) recently
Energy/Oil & Gas in focus with Brent at $111 — upstream beneficiaries vs OMC margin pressure
Metals: Vedanta earnings expected to show 20-25% revenue growth on better zinc/aluminium realisations
risk_flags: Iran-US conflict ongoing; Strait of Hormuz blockaded since Feb 28 2026 — vessel traffic at ~5% of normal
Brent crude surged to ~$111/bbl (highest since March), WTI ~$99 — every $10 oil rise adds $15B to India import bill and ~60bps to CPI
USD/INR breached ₹94 — rupee at 4-week low ~94.2 amid oil shock and FII outflows
Rubio rejected Iran's offer to reopen Strait if US lifts blockade — diplomatic standoff continues
FIIs have pulled $45B over 18 months — sustained foreign outflow risk
Imported inflation risk threatens RBI's neutral stance and could pressure rate cut hopes
Trade deficit concerns widening with oil spike
Hawkish BoJ stance adds to global risk-off tone
brief_summary: Negative setup for Indian markets today: weak US close (chip-led Nasdaq selloff), oil at $111+ on Hormuz blockade, rupee at record low ~94.2, and Sensex/Nifty already broke below 24,000 yesterday. Watch heavy earnings docket (Adani Power, Bajaj Finance, Vedanta, Federal Bank) for stock-specific moves while broader market remains hostage to oil and FII outflows. Bias defensive — favor IT (rupee tailwind), select metals (Vedanta), and avoid OMCs/aviation/auto until oil stabilizes.
